macro_events.title: CBK emergency hike to 10%
macro_events.description: CBK raises benchmark rate to 10.0% in May then 11.5% in July to defend KES and combat imported inflation as shilling approaches 100/USD

macro_events.title: CBK hike to 11.5%
macro_events.description: CBK raises benchmark rate to 11.5% to defend KES and combat imported inflation as shilling hits 100/USD

macro_events.title: Kenya GDP revision
macro_events.description: Kenya's GDP revised upward by 25% following rebasing of national accounts; Kenya reclassified as lower-middle-income economy

macro_events.title: Interest Rate Cap Law
macro_events.description: Banking (Amendment) Act 2016 caps lending rates at CBK rate + 4%; severely disrupts credit growth and causes banks to shun SME and personal lending

macro_events.title: Re-run election
macro_events.description: Supreme Court nullifies August 2017 presidential election; Uhuru Kenyatta wins uncontested re-run; prolonged political uncertainty impacts market sentiment and investment

macro_events.title: Rate Cap Partial Repeal
macro_events.description: Parliament partially repeals rate cap floor on deposits; CBK signals further liberalisation of interest rate framework

macro_events.title: Rate Cap Full Repeal
macro_events.description: National Assembly repeals interest rate cap law entirely; banks return to risk-based pricing; credit growth expected to recover

macro_events.title: COVID-19 First Case
macro_events.description: Kenya confirms first COVID-19 case; government announces containment measures including curfews, school closures and border restrictions

macro_events.title: COVID Emergency Cut
macro_events.description: CBK makes emergency 100bps rate cut to 7.25% in response to COVID-19; banks ordered to restructure distressed loans; moratorium on loan classification issued

macro_events.title: COVID tax reliefs
macro_events.description: Government announces 100% income tax relief for lowest earners, VAT reduction from 16% to 14%, and corporate tax cut to 25% amid COVID-19 economic impact

macro_events.title: IMF Facility
macro_events.description: Kenya secures USD 2.3bn IMF Extended Fund Facility and Extended Credit Facility amid COVID-related debt overhang; structural reforms agreed

macro_events.title: Russia-Ukraine War
macro_events.description: Russia invades Ukraine; global food and fuel prices spike sharply, raising Kenya's import cost pressures; cooking oil, wheat and fertiliser prices surge

macro_events.title: Rate Tightening Begins
macro_events.description: CBK begins tightening cycle with 50bps hike to 7.5% as global inflation rises post-Russia-Ukraine war; first hike since 2015

macro_events.title: Kenya General Election
macro_events.description: William Ruto wins Kenya's presidential election; peaceful democratic transition; markets cautious but stable; Ruto pledges bottom-up economic model

macro_events.title: Finance Bill 2023 Protests
macro_events.description: Mass protests across Kenya against Finance Bill 2023 proposed tax hikes including housing levy and fuel taxes; government withdraws controversial clauses after demonstrations

macro_events.title: Housing Levy
macro_events.description: Government introduces 1.5% Housing Levy on gross salary deducted from employees and matched by employers; controversy over constitutionality and affordability

macro_events.title: CBK shock hike to 12.5%
macro_events.description: CBK delivers shock 200bps hike to 12.5% as KES approaches record low of 150/USD; front-loaded tightening to anchor inflation expectations and defend currency

macro_events.title: KES recovers from record low
macro_events.description: Kenya shilling stabilises near 150/USD after touching all-time weakest levels around 157/USD; CBK intervention and improved dollar inflows support recovery

macro_events.title: Eurobond repayment
macro_events.description: Kenya successfully repays USD 2bn Eurobond maturing March 2024 via new issuance and IMF support; market confidence in Kenya's fiscal management improves significantly

macro_events.title: Finance Bill 2024 Protests
macro_events.description: Gen Z-led mass protests against Finance Bill 2024 tax proposals; parliament stormed on June 25; government withdraws entire Finance Bill; fiscal consolidation plans revised

macro_events.title: IMF Review
macro_events.description: IMF completes Kenya review; approves USD 600mn disbursement under ongoing programme; fiscal consolidation deemed on track despite Finance Bill withdrawal

macro_events.title: CBK easing cycle begins
macro_events.description: CBK makes first rate cut since 2020, reducing rate 25bps to 12.75%; inflation below 5% and KES recovered to 130/USD support the pivot

macro_events.title: CBK easing accelerates
macro_events.description: CBK cuts rate 75bps to 12.0%, signalling faster easing cycle as inflation falls below target and KES stabilises; financial conditions improving

macro_events.title: KES appreciation
macro_events.description: KES strengthens past 130/USD from 2023 crisis lows as fiscal position improves, diaspora remittances surge to record USD 4.9bn in 2024, and investor confidence returns

macro_events.title: CBK reaches neutral
macro_events.description: CBK cuts to 9.5%, approaching estimated neutral rate of 8.5-9.0%; signals data-dependent approach going forward; easing cycle largely complete
